message:
fix(config): replace NODE_OPTIONS with cgroup memory limits for native binaries

Claude Code (v2.1.69+) is a native ELF binary compiled with bun, not
a Node.js process. NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=..." was silently
ignored, leaving agents with no memory limit and causing frequent OOM
kills (up to 60GB per agent on 64GB machines).

New approach:
- memLimitPrefix template func returns "systemd-run --user --scope -q
  -p MemoryMax=XM" on Linux (real cgroup v2 enforcement)
- Falls back to empty string on macOS/other (no OS-level limit available)
- memLimitMB computes 25% of system RAM, clamped 2048-16384 MB
- nodeHeapMB kept as deprecated alias for backward compatibility with
  user-customized templates

Closes #84

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1027,7 +1027,7 @@ projects_base = "~/Developer"
 
 [agents]
 # Commands used to launch each agent type
-claude = 'NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=32768" claude --dangerously-skip-permissions'
+claude = '{{memLimitPrefix}} claude --dangerously-skip-permissions'
 codex = "codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -m gpt-5.1-codex-max"
 gemini = "gemini --yolo"
